findings.description: Off-by-one pagination: offset = page * page_size with 1-based pages skips the entire first page, so page 1 returns page 2's data; correct formula is (page - 1) * page_size.
name_pattern is compiled and executed as an attacker-controlled regex with no validation or timeout, enabling ReDoS catastrophic-backtracking denial of service.
Every search fetches all non-deleted users into memory then filters/sorts/paginates in Python with no SQL LIMIT/OFFSET, giving O(N) memory and CPU per request regardless of page size.
page_size and page have no lower-bound clamp, so zero or negative values produce negative offsets and Python negative-index slicing silently returns wrong windows.
matcher.search(u['display_name']) raises TypeError if display_name is NULL in the database since there is no null-guard on the column value.
No error handling around connect/execute; sqlite3.OperationalError (locked DB, missing file, disk full) propagates raw to callers with no graceful degradation.
departments() returns NULL as a list element when any user has a null department, since SELECT DISTINCT department does not exclude NULLs.
